categories.mysterious-name: a name that doesn't reveal what it does/holds
categories.duplicated-code: the same logic shape in more than one place in the change
categories.feature-envy: a method that reaches into another object's data more than its own
categories.data-clumps: the same few fields/params keep travelling together
categories.primitive-obsession: a primitive/string standing in for a domain concept
categories.repeated-switches: the same switch/if-cascade on the same type recurs
categories.shotgun-surgery: one logical change forces scattered edits across many files
categories.divergent-change: one module edited for several unrelated reasons
categories.speculative-generality: abstraction/params/hooks added for needs the spec doesn't have
categories.message-chains: long a.b().c().d() navigation the caller shouldn't depend on
categories.middle-man: a class/function that mostly just delegates onward
categories.refused-bequest: a subclass that ignores/overrides most of what it inherits
categories.spec-missing: a required behaviour is absent or only partially implemented
categories.spec-scope-creep: behaviour added that the spec did not ask for

forge.mutation_task: You are improving a SKILL.md an agent uses to REVIEW a code diff and detect defects along two axes: Standards (Fowler code smells) and Spec (does the diff match the ticket/PRD). A better SKILL.md helps the reviewer catch real planted defects while NOT inventing findings on clean diffs.
mutation_angles: Sharpen each smell's definition and add one crisp positive + negative example, so the reviewer recognizes it in a diff without over-flagging.
Add a short decision procedure the reviewer runs per hunk, and tighten the spec-vs-diff comparison (missing vs scope-creep vs wrong).
Reduce false positives: state explicitly what each smell is NOT, with counter-examples of clean code that resembles it.
Add a compact checklist the reviewer applies before reporting, ordered by how often each defect is missed.
